text.title: product-operations Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for product-operations as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: PM agent getting write token "for convenience" and starting to edit Linear tickets directly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Once an agent has write access and uses it without triggering an error, the habit is established; audit token scopes per agent in CI and refuse new writer tokens without a Product Ops co-sign.

failure-mode.description: Shadow rollups. PM subagents that read Linear/Jira/Productboard directly (instead of the Product Ops canonical store) produce numbers that diverge from the org-published figures. The PM ships a deck, Product Ops ships a different deck, trust collapses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always read from the canonical store.

failure-mode.description: Charter drift. When a PM's own subagent gets good at status packs, the PM is tempted to skip Product Ops. Six months later the org has two parallel rollup systems and no one owns reconciliation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hard rule: PM-side agents read, Product Ops agents write.

failure-mode.description: Decision laundering. "Product Ops said the feature is at risk, so we killed it" — this offloads accountability from the PM to a function that has no decision rights.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents must label every Product Ops output as signal, never recommendation-to-kill.

failure-mode.description: Template fragmentation. PM uses brainstorm to design a new spec template, ships it inside their squad, never hands it to Product Ops. Three squads later there are three spec templates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: Maturity mismatch. PM asks for AI-driven predictive capacity, Product Ops is at Level 1 (process docs only). The PM-side agent must detect and refuse the request, pointing to the maturity gap, not silently produce a fragile prediction.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Maturity mismatch becomes technical debt when a capability is built on top of immature infrastructure.

failure-mode.description: OKR-from-Product-Ops trap. Product Ops can roll up OKR progress; it cannot author meaningful OKRs. PMs who ask their subagent to "use Product Ops to write our OKRs" get cargo-culted documents.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OKRs need strategic context the rollup does not contain; require human sign-off on every PM-authored OKR.

failure-mode.description: Agent saying "Product Ops recommends killing feature F-123" — recommendation laundering.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Product Ops provides signal, not recommendations; recommendations without decision authority confuse accountability. Strip "Product Ops recommends" language at the prompt level; only the PM recommends.

failure-mode.description: PM posts a "draft" directly into Notion canonical pages because the hand-off queue felt like ceremony.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The queue is the contract, not ceremony; lock the canonical pages at the API level so "drafts" cannot bypass the queue even when the PM considers them harmless.

failure-mode.description: Stale rollup as fresh data. PM agent reads rollup-latest.md whose last_updated is 5 days old, presents it in an exec deck.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always read the freshness header and refuse to compose stakeholder content on stale inputs.

failure-mode.description: Cargo-cult OKRs from rollups. LLM sees "feature X regressed by 12%" and proposes an OKR "improve X by 20%."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OKRs need strategic context the rollup does not contain. Require human sign-off on every PM-authored OKR.

failure-mode.description: Cross-system ID drift in PM artefacts. PM memo cites "feature 123" ambiguously across Productboard / Jira / Linear IDs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always resolve through the canonical mapping table Product Ops maintains.

failure-mode.description: Privacy contamination. PM pulls customer interview transcripts into the same context as Product Ops rollups; PII leaks into the metrics store.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Keep PM narrative store and Product Ops metrics store on separate auth domains.

failure-mode.description: Charter ambiguity coded into prompts. A prompt that says "use Product Ops or just query the data yourself" trains the agent to bypass the function.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Prompts must be unambiguous: read from canonical store, hand off to Product Ops, full stop.

failure-mode.description: Maturity-skip silence. Agent silently produces a Level 3 predictive output even though Product Ops is at Level 1.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Make the maturity check a hard gate, not a soft warning.
